## Appendix — confirmed findings (full detail)

### F1 [high] Reading-order verifier exists but the high-risk AI fix loop never calls it — aiFixChunked accepts on length/char-count floors only and joins chunks blindly

- **Area:** Remediation core / content fidelity (b0d24ae3 reading-order scar)
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** checkReadingOrderPreserved at :509 is called ONLY by _restyleGate :601 (and exported :27709) — grep confirms no other callers. aiFixChunked per-chunk accept gate at :2950 (out.length>=part.length*0.9 && textCharCount(out)>=textCharCount(part)*0.95); fixed.join('') at :3030; aggregate floor _AGG_TEXT_FLOOR=0.85 at :3024-3025; acceptFixedHtml (single-chunk) is size/marker-only.
- **Detail:** The genuine subsequence/order guard checkReadingOrderPreserved is wired ONLY into the three tiny S3 block-restyle helpers (the SAFEST path). The highest-volume remediation path — aiFixChunked, driving every Step-4 auto-fix pass — has NO order or content-equality check. Its per-chunk accept gate is purely magnitude-based (90% length, 95% text chars), so an AI pass that reorders blocks (e.g. moves a heading after its paragraph, reflows a list, re-interleaves columns) is accepted as long as char totals hold. Chunks are then concatenated by index with fixed.join('') — no re-parse, no cross-chunk context. The only aggregate backstop is a 0.85 text floor, tolerating ~15% silent total text loss before the whole pass reverts. None of the four downstream integrity nets (char-coverage ~97% floor, word-Set, numeric multiset, structural notes) are order-sensitive, so nothing catches a reorder. This is the single biggest UMaine-credibility risk and the fix (calling the existing function on fix output) is small.
- **Trigger:** Remediate any multi-section / multi-column document where one AI fix pass reorders blocks or linearizes columns in the wrong interleave. Char counts are preserved, so the pass is accepted and shipped with no warning; the AT reading order is scrambled and the score stays high.
- **Suggested fix:** Call checkReadingOrderPreserved(prevHtml, fixedHtml) inside acceptFixedHtml and in aiFixChunked's per-chunk accept path (revert/keep-original or surface a reading-order WARN on ok===false), the same discipline _restyleGate already uses. The function already exists and is exported.

### F2 [high] Tagged-PDF reading order is never verified: Stage-4 uses order-blind index pairing and the only post-save text check is a token SET (order-insensitive) — a scrambled tag tree ships with 100% coverage and still earns PDF/UA-1

- **Area:** Tagged-PDF output / reading order + PDF/UA claim
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** _stage4_matchSegmentsToItems pure index pairing on equal counts at :6203-6206; round-trip text diff builds new Set(_normalize(...)) for _origTokens/_shipTokens at :20786-20787 and compares membership at :20794; PDF/UA-1 declaration gated on orphan-linkage/font-embed/contentDropped, not order.
- **Detail:** Two reinforcing facts make tagged-PDF reading order invisible to every gate. (1) When segments.length===items.length, _stage4_matchSegmentsToItems index-pairs BT/ET content-stream segments to pdf.js items (:6203-6206); BT byte order != pdf.js reading order for multi-column or out-of-visual-order content streams, so role/ActualText/MCID attach to the wrong block and the tree's reading order is wrong (the nearest-neighbour fallback only fires on a count MISMATCH). (2) The only post-save text check builds new Set() of >=3-char tokens and compares set membership (:20786) — explicitly order-insensitive — so a mis-ordered tree yields ~100% coverage and a green round-trip. The PDF/UA-1 XMP declaration is written on orphan-linkage + font embedding + contentDropped, none of which inspect order. Net: AlloFlow can write pdfuaid:part=1 on a PDF whose logical reading order is scrambled (a PDF/UA-1 §7.2 violation) with every shipped gate green. Goldens are single-column page-1 fixtures, so multi-column order is untested.
- **Trigger:** Remediate a two-column PDF (journal/newsletter/two-column syllabus) to a tagged PDF where the content stream draws the right column's BT objects before the left. The SR reading order is scrambled but token coverage is 100%, the round-trip passes, and the PDF/UA chip stays green.
- **Suggested fix:** Add an ORDER-SENSITIVE post-save check: compare original deterministic reading-order token SEQUENCE vs the assembled tag-tree leaf SEQUENCE (LCS ratio, not set membership), and withhold pdfuaid:part=1 + raise a reading-order banner on divergence. For Stage-4, validate index-paired items are monotonic in (y,x) before trusting the equal-count fast path; otherwise fall to nearest-neighbour / sort-by-reading-position. Pin a multi-column golden.

### F3 [high] aiFixChunked splits HTML on a bare '>' boundary, cutting mid-table/mid-list; isolated chunks are fixed then naively join('')'d with no tag-balance check

- **Area:** Remediation core (chunk splitting + join)
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** splitHtmlOnTagBoundary at :2402 backs up only to last '>' (:2410); per-chunk gate is char-count only (:2950); half-split fallback loosens to 0.85/0.9 (:2954-2968); fixed.join('') at :3030; aggregate floor 0.85 at :3024.
- **Detail:** splitHtmlOnTagBoundary only backs up to the last '>' before the size limit, so it freely splits between </td> and <td> or </li> and <li>, cutting a <table>/<ul> across two chunks. Each chunk is sent to Gemini in isolation (nothing enforces tag balance), fixed independently, and concatenated with fixed.join('') — no re-parse, no tag-balance/table-cell-count assertion. A boundary inside a table can duplicate a header row (one half re-emits it for context), drop continuation rows, or orphan a <td>, none of which the char-count per-chunk gate sees. The sole backstop is the aggregate 0.85 text floor, tolerating ~15% silent total text loss.
- **Trigger:** Remediate a document with a table or long list larger than HTML_FIX_CHUNK (16000 chars). The split lands inside the table; one half re-emits the header (duplication) or the other drops continuation rows (loss). Char totals stay above 0.85 so it ships.
- **Suggested fix:** Make splitHtmlOnTagBoundary split only at top-level block boundaries (track open-tag depth; never inside table/list/figure), or merge and re-fix a tag-unbalanced chunk pair. At minimum run a tag-balance + table-cell-count assertion on each joined result. Route multi-chunk output through acceptFixedHtmlDetailed's structural/fabrication checks (currently single-chunk only).

### F4 [high] Plain designMode typing and Ctrl+heading/link/list execCommands in the preview are silently lost on Save & Close and every alt-format export

- **Area:** Remediation UI — dual source of truth (live iframe vs stored accessibleHtml)
- **File:** view_pdf_audit_source.jsx (+ AlloFlowANTI.txt)
- **Location:** Preview iframe onLoad (:14111) binds click (:14121), keydown execCommand for h1/h2/h3/link/lists (:14133-14143), and pointer events — but NO 'input'/'keyup' listener calling the sync. Only live->stored writeback is __alloflowOnPdfPreviewMutated, fired only by explicit image/table/insert handlers. saveProjectToFile/_saveAndCloseAudit read the STORED pdfFixResultRef.current.accessibleHtml; _dlOdt/_dlDaisy/_dlEpubMO and the HTML export read pdfFixResult.accessibleHtml directly.
- **Detail:** The preview runs designMode='on' so a teacher can type corrections directly into the document, and the keydown handler itself runs doc.execCommand('formatBlock','<h2>')/createLink/insertOrderedList on Ctrl+1/2/3/0/K/L — real structural edits. But raw typing and those execCommands fire no DOM-mutation signal the app listens for, so the stored accessibleHtml string stays at its last programmatically-set value. Durable persistence and most exports read the stored string, not the live DOM (only a few paths harvest getPdfPreviewHtml()). So a teacher who types fixes (alt text, corrected words, a new heading via Ctrl+2) and clicks Save & Close or exports to HTML/ODT/DAISY/EPUB loses all of it. safeCloseAudit's hasWork check also reads accessibleHtml, so it can't even detect the unsaved live work to prompt.
- **Trigger:** Open a remediated doc, type a correction (or press Ctrl+2 for a heading / Ctrl+K for a link) in the preview, then click Save & Close (or Download HTML / ODT / DAISY / EPUB). The typed/shortcut edits are gone.
- **Suggested fix:** Before any persistence/export path that reads accessibleHtml directly, harvest the live DOM first via getPdfPreviewHtml() and commit it (the pattern already used at :11008). Or bind an 'input'/'blur' listener inside the iframe onLoad that calls the debounced __alloflowOnPdfPreviewMutated so designMode typing and the keydown execCommands sync like the explicit mutations do.

### F5 [high] Cross-document state bleed: results-screen Load Project never unmounts PdfAuditView, so palette Revert can overwrite the newly loaded document with the PREVIOUS document's HTML

- **Area:** Remediation UI — multi-session / component lifecycle
- **File:** view_pdf_audit_source.jsx (+ AlloFlowANTI.txt)
- **Location:** PdfAuditView rendered with NO key prop, conditional on (pdfAuditResult || pdfAuditLoading) (AlloFlowANTI.txt:27567). Results-screen Load-Project onChange (:9716-9792) sets a fresh pdfFixResult but never nulls pdfAuditResult → no remount. _paletteSnapshotRef (:1937) reset only inside _revertPalette (:3234); _appliedPalette/_issueEdit/tagOutline/_restyleProposals have no per-document reset.
- **Detail:** The component is implicitly designed to reset between documents by UNMOUNTING (the startNewPdfAudit path nulls pdfAuditResult → real remount). But the results-screen Load Project swaps in a new pdfFixResult object while leaving pdfAuditResult truthy, so React keeps the same mounted instance and its refs survive. Concrete corruption: (1) apply a palette to doc A → _paletteSnapshotRef.current = A's pre-palette HTML, _appliedPalette set; (2) Load Project doc B (no remount); (3) palette toolbar still shows the preset active and pressing Revert runs _revertPalette → setPdfFixResult(p=>({...p, accessibleHtml: snap})) where snap is A's HTML, then _reauditAndScore(snap) — silently replacing B's content with A's and reporting a score for the wrong document. Stale _issueEdit drafts, tagOutline, and _restyleProposals (whose originals are A's block outerHTML) also leak into B.
- **Trigger:** Apply a palette (or open an issue editor / build the tag outline) on one document, then use the results-screen 'Load Project' to open a different .alloflow.json without using 'Start new'/closing, then press the palette Revert button.
- **Suggested fix:** Give PdfAuditView a key prop tied to document identity (forces remount on Load Project), OR null pdfAuditResult during load, OR add a per-document reset effect keyed on a stable document id that clears _paletteSnapshotRef/_appliedPalette/_issueEdit/tagOutline/_restyleProposals/_lastTaggedBytesRef. The existing mediaMode/pdfMetaOverride per-file effects are the template.

### F6 [high] _reauditAndScore has no run-generation / stale-HTML guard — a second edit during an in-flight re-audit makes the displayed score and remaining-issue list attest to bytes no longer stored

- **Area:** Scoring/Audit honesty — concurrency (score must describe the shipped doc)
- **File:** view_pdf_audit_source.jsx
- **Location:** _reauditAndScore (:2850-2873): awaits Promise.all([auditOutputAccessibility, runAxeAudit]) then setPdfFixResult(prev => ({...prev, afterScore, verificationAudit, axeAudit, issueResolution})) WITHOUT re-setting accessibleHtml and with no token. Called fire-and-forget from manual edit (:2949), scoped intent (:2989), restyle (:3103), proposal (:3157), palette (:3206), revertPalette (:3237), Workbench (:10194). Contrast runAutoFixLoop's _myRunGen guard (AlloFlowANTI.txt:15888).
- **Detail:** _reauditAndScore(newHtml) runs two async audits then folds afterScore/verificationAudit/axeAudit/issueResolution onto prev, preserving prev.accessibleHtml (it trusts the caller already stored newHtml). There is no generation token and no check that prev.accessibleHtml still equals newHtml. If a second targeted edit lands while the first re-audit is awaiting Gemini: edit#1 stores HTML#1 + kicks re-audit#1; edit#2 stores HTML#2; re-audit#1 resolves and writes a score computed from HTML#1 onto a state whose accessibleHtml is now HTML#2. The headline score and remaining-issues list then describe HTML#1 while the exported/tagged document is HTML#2 — precisely the measurement-honesty failure the project is hardened against. The auto-fix loop has this guard (_myRunGen); the interactive edit paths do not.
- **Trigger:** Apply a palette preset, then quickly (before the 'Updated: N/100' toast) apply a different preset or a region restyle. The score/issue panel can settle on the first edit's audit while accessibleHtml holds the second edit.
- **Suggested fix:** Capture the newHtml (or a monotonically-increasing edit token) at entry and in the terminal setPdfFixResult bail when prev.accessibleHtml !== newHtml (or the token is stale): prev => (prev && prev.accessibleHtml === newHtml) ? {...} : prev. Mirror runAutoFixLoop's _myRunGen discipline.

### F7 [high] Stale OCR window globals leak the PRIOR scanned doc's text into the next born-digital doc's AutoRestore and mislabel it scanned

- **Area:** Remediation core / cross-document state leak
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** Step-1 reset block (:13448-13454) resets __lastGroundTruth*/__lastOcrPageErrors/__lastOcrLowConfidencePages but NOT __lastOcrTesseractText/__lastOcrVisionText/__lastOcrDisagreements/__lastOcrMethod. Those are assigned only in the dual-OCR path (:13879-13882). End-of-run AutoRestore reads _arTess/_arVis from these globals (:16602-16603); isScanned derived from __lastOcrMethod (:14346).
- **Detail:** Every run resets the ground-truth globals but NOT the four OCR text/method globals, which are only set inside the scanned dual-OCR path. A born-digital (text-layer) PDF never enters that path, so it inherits the previous scanned document's values. Two failures: (1) the end-of-run dual-OCR AutoRestore gates on _arTess && _arVis read from these globals; for a born-digital doc both are still truthy (prior doc's OCR), so the gate passes, then any current-source token absent from the current final but present in the PRIOR doc's OCR sets is treated as a 'dropped word' to re-splice or park in the Content-recovery appendix — injecting spurious/duplicate words into a document that lost nothing. (2) The extraction-complete event reports isScanned from __lastOcrMethod (:14346), so the born-digital doc is mislabeled 'scanned' and shows the prior doc's Tesseract-vs-Vision disagreements.
- **Trigger:** In one Canvas session, remediate a scanned PDF (runs dual-OCR, sets the globals), then remediate a born-digital text-layer PDF. The second doc's output gets the first doc's words restored/appended and is flagged scanned with stale disagreements.
- **Suggested fix:** Reset window.__lastOcrTesseractText=''; __lastOcrVisionText=''; __lastOcrDisagreements=[]; __lastOcrMethod=null; in the Step-1 reset block at ~13448-13454 alongside the other per-run globals.

### F8 [high] Image-extraction pdf.js document is never destroy()'d — worker/transport leaks on every image-bearing PDF remediation

- **Area:** Remediation core / pdf.js worker lifecycle leak
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** Step-1b image extraction declares `const pdfDoc = await ...getDocument(...)` INSIDE the try at :14067; used at 14088/14090; block has no finally{ pdfDoc.destroy() } (confirmed: no destroy/finally in 14067-14300). Every other getDocument site (:2572,:5394,:5595,:6639,:6719,:19051) releases in a finally.
- **Detail:** Every other getDocument call in the file hoists the doc and releases it in finally{ try{pdf.destroy()}catch{} }. The image-extraction path uniquely declares pdfDoc inside the try and never destroys it on any path (success, per-page error, or outer catch). A pdf.js doc holds a worker port/transport GC doesn't reliably reclaim, so each image-bearing remediation leaks one worker doc for the session. The surrounding code is explicitly fighting Canvas-iframe OOM (it zeroes canvases and resets __pdfPageCanvases at :14083) — so this is the same resource-leak class the author was trying to eliminate, left on the doc object itself, a likely contributor to multi-doc-session 'doesn't work every time' reports.
- **Trigger:** Remediate several image-bearing PDFs in one Canvas session; each leaves a live pdf.js worker doc. Accumulated workers/transports drive memory growth and eventual mid-pipeline failure.
- **Suggested fix:** Hoist `let pdfDoc = null;` above the try and add `finally { if (pdfDoc) { try { pdfDoc.destroy(); } catch(_){} pdfDoc = null; } }` around the image-extraction block, matching every other getDocument site.

### F9 [high] A PARTIAL final content audit (chunk loss below the 25% NULL threshold) governs the headline afterScore as a complete 'content' score with no asterisk and no incomplete flag

- **Area:** Scoring/Audit honesty (final-audit partial coverage hidden on the headline)
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** _coverageTooLow gate at :9979 only NULLs when (_failedChunks/chunks.length)>0.25 && (_failedChunks>=2 || _auditedCount<2). _aiDegraded explicitly NOT keyed off _partialAudit (comment :16542; _aiDegraded def :16548). Result object never carries the final audit's _partialAudit flag; headline rendered as plain 'content' at view :7215-7217.
- **Detail:** The partial-audit floor only NULLs the AI score when coverage drops below 75%. A final audit that loses 1 of 6 chunks (17%) is _partialAudit:true but keeps a REAL, artificially-high score (fewer sections audited → fewer violations found → higher score, the inflation the code documents). _aiDegraded is FALSE for it (score is a real number), so it flows into _alloComputeHeadline as a full content layer with _aiVerificationIncomplete false and _scoreSource='min'. The result object never carries _partialAudit onto the headline, and the dashboard renders it as plain emerald 'content' with no asterisk (the throttle/'structural only' disclosure is gated solely on _aiVerificationIncomplete). The per-chunk stream and audit summary string disclose partial coverage, but the headline number a reviewer reads does not.
- **Trigger:** Remediate a long doc (>16KB → chunked final audit) during a mild throttle that drops 1 chunk of a 6-chunk audit (17% < 25%). Headline shows e.g. '92/100 content' with no partial-coverage marker though 1/6 was never re-audited.
- **Suggested fix:** Propagate the final audit's _partialAudit/chunksAudited/chunksRequested onto the result object and either render an asterisk + tooltip ('headline covers N/M audited sections — re-run for full coverage'), or treat _partialAudit on the FINAL authoritative audit as _aiVerificationIncomplete regardless of the 25% threshold, since the headline is no longer reproducible from a full pass.

### F10 [high] Companion (plain-language / translation) staleness is detected by character LENGTH only — any equal-length edit leaves a stale companion presented as current, surviving save/load

- **Area:** Export / companions staleness
- **File:** view_pdf_audit_source.jsx
- **Location:** Staleness badge render at :14000 (_plainLanguage) and the sibling _translation render: `srcLen != null && srcLen !== (pdfFixResult.accessibleHtml||'').length`. srcLen captured at generation (:10615 translation, :10692 plain-language) as accessibleHtml.length. A robust freshness signal (_userEditedAt: Date.now()) is already stamped on every edit path (:2149/2470/2661/6031/6651/10560/11008/13973) and ignored.
- **Detail:** The 'out of date — regenerate' badge trips only when the stored character count srcLen differs from current accessibleHtml.length — a pure length-equality test. So any edit that preserves document length leaves a genuinely-stale companion flagged fresh: a one-char typo fix, an equal-length word swap, a palette change (hex swap is same-length), a region restyle swapping an equal-length class/inline value, or two compensating edits. The companion persists into the saved .alloflow.json and is restored with the same length-blind check, so a stale translation survives a save/load round-trip looking current. For a school psychologist handing a translated IEP/notice to a non-English family, a silently-stale translation is an equity/credibility hazard. The companion already stores at:Date.now() and _userEditedAt exists on every edit — a real timestamp signal is present but unused.
- **Trigger:** Generate a plain-language or translation companion, then make any same-length edit (replace a word with an equal-length word, or apply a palette). The companion modal shows no 'out of date' badge though it no longer matches the source.
- **Suggested fix:** Compare the companion's stored at timestamp against pdfFixResult._userEditedAt (stale when _userEditedAt > companion.at), or store and compare a content hash of accessibleHtml, instead of comparing string length.

### F11 [medium] OCR'd document text (potential student PII) is written to the copyable in-app diagnostics buffer

- **Area:** Security / FERPA — PII in telemetry buffer
- **File:** gemini_api_source.jsx
- **Location:** callGeminiVision truncation-recovery branch :615: warnLog('[Vision] Recovered partial text:', partial.substring(0, 100)). Sink: warnLog pushes into window.__alloDiagLog ring buffer (AlloFlowANTI.txt:511-524), surfaced + copyable via PdfDiagnosticsLog (view_pdf_audit_source.jsx:1698).
- **Detail:** warnLog/debugLog don't just hit the console — every call also pushes its concatenated args into window.__alloDiagLog, a 1000-entry ring buffer rendered by the PdfDiagnosticsLog panel so a teacher can SEE and COPY pipeline diagnostics from inside Canvas. Line 615 logs the first 100 chars of the Vision model's recovered transcription on a truncated response. On scanned IEPs/psych evals/504 plans, those first 100 chars are typically raw student record content (name, DOB, diagnosis in a header). It now sits in a buffer the teacher is invited to copy/paste into a bug report or share with the maintainer. The FERPA posture permits content egress to Gemini, but NOT replication of record content into a sharable diagnostics artifact. Bounded at 100 chars, but the leak class is real and runs on the most sensitive path (OCR of scanned records).
- **Trigger:** Upload a scanned IEP whose Vision OCR response is truncated (long page / MAX_TOKENS) so callGeminiVision takes the partial-extraction recovery branch; open the Diagnostics panel and Copy — the buffer contains the document's opening text.
- **Suggested fix:** Log only the recovered LENGTH, never content: warnLog('[Vision] Recovered partial text:', partial.length + ' chars'). Same treatment for any other content-bearing warnLog/debugLog; optionally redact obvious PII patterns before any string enters __alloPushLog.

### F12 [medium] Step-2 chunk RECITATION/parse-failure fallback recovers text by char-proportional slicing of extractedText, mislocating recovered content

- **Area:** Remediation core (Step-2 JSON build fallbacks)
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** char-proportional slice fallbacks at ~15029-15031 (throw path) and ~15141-15143 (non-throw path): extractedText.substring(floor(i/transformChunks*len), floor((i+1)/transformChunks*len)).
- **Detail:** When a Step-2 Vision JSON chunk throws (RECITATION) or returns unparseable content, recovery slices the page's text from extractedText by chunk-index PROPORTION. Each chunk is 3 pages (TRANSFORM_PAGES=3) but text density varies wildly across pages, so the proportional slice does not align to the failed chunk's actual page range — the recovered text can be pulled from the wrong region of the document, displaced relative to its true position, with no detector (char-coverage and numeric nets are order-blind and pass). Successful chunks ARE concatenated in correct ci order, so this is a per-failed-chunk misplacement, not a global reorder.
- **Trigger:** Remediate a copyrighted-text PDF where the middle 3-page chunk triggers RECITATION while earlier pages are text-dense and later sparse. The proportional slice for the failed chunk grabs text belonging to an adjacent chunk's pages.
- **Suggested fix:** Track the per-chunk character span of extractedText using window.__lastGroundTruthPageMap so the fallback slices by actual page boundaries, not uniform chunk-index proportion.

### F13 [low] Tier 2.5 section-scoped AI repair targets heading-order/page-has-heading-one/region/bypass, but runAxeAudit never emits those best-practice rule IDs — that repair branch is dead

- **Area:** Scoring/audit gap (axe best-practice) + structural repair
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** runAxeAudit runOnly excludes best-practice (values: wcag2a/2aa/21a/21aa) at :4647 and :10108. TIER2_5_RULE_IDS includes 'heading-order'/'page-has-heading-one'/region/bypass (:4803-4811); clusterAxeViolationsBySection filters on those ids (:4847); orchestrator fed bestAxe=runAxeAudit(html) (view_pdf_audit_source.jsx ~5530).
- **Detail:** axe classifies heading-order, page-has-heading-one, region, bypass, landmark-* as BEST-PRACTICE, excluded by runAxeAudit's runOnly tag filter. Tier 2.5 (the section-scoped AI repair built to fix heading hierarchy / landmark structure) selects clusters by filtering axe violations whose id is in TIER2_5_RULE_IDS — whose headline entries are exactly those excluded best-practice rules. The orchestrator is fed runAxeAudit(html), which by construction can never contain them, so those branches are unreachable; only WCAG-tagged ids in the set (duplicate-id, aria-required-children/parent) ever cluster. The deterministic _collapseHeadingSkipsStrict and the AI rubric's HEADINGS item partially compensate, but the advertised section-scoped AI heading-hierarchy repair is effectively dead and the automated score layer is blind to scrambled heading levels / missing h1.
- **Trigger:** Run 'Fix Remaining Issues' on a doc with an h1->h3 skip inside one <section>. Tier 2.5 logs 'considering 0 clusters' because heading-order never appears in bestAxe; the section-scoped AI fix never runs.
- **Suggested fix:** Run a second axe pass with best-practice rules enabled (or add the specific rule ids via runOnly id list) to feed Tier 2.5's cluster selection, OR stop advertising heading-order/page-has-heading-one/region/bypass in TIER2_5_RULE_IDS so the deterministic backstop is the documented sole owner.

### F14 [medium] Deterministic alt-text fallback injects generic alt='Image' / 'Document image' that clears axe image-alt by presence while providing zero accessibility value

- **Area:** Alt text / measurement honesty
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** Step-2 BUILD path stamps alt='Document image' at :15775; HTML-engine Phase 3b defaults to 'Image'; axe-target fixer uses p.alt || 'Image'. Contrast the honest surgical path 'Image (needs description)' at :12062 and the _altTrivial diagnostic at :20513-20522.
- **Detail:** Two deterministic alt-injection sites on the PRIMARY deliverable inject a meaningless generic alt that clears axe's image-alt rule (presence-only) without giving a screen-reader user anything: Step-2 build stamps alt='Document image' (:15775) and Phase 3b falls back to 'Image'. These fire before/instead of the Vision alt pass when Vision was skipped, throttled, or classified the image as undescribable. The automated (axe) score reports image-alt resolved and the headline can read high, while a PAC 2024 / NVDA review immediately flags 'uninformative alt'. The codebase already knows the honest pattern — the surgical fixer writes 'Image (needs description)' and diagnostics flag _altTrivial — so this is an inconsistency where the high-traffic deterministic paths overclaim by presence.
- **Trigger:** Remediate a figure-heavy PDF while the Vision alt pass is throttled (429), or route through the HTML 'Make Accessible' engine. Every figure ships alt='Document image'; axe image-alt shows 0 violations; a human hears 'Document image, Document image, Document image'.
- **Suggested fix:** Make the deterministic fallback honest like the surgical path: inject alt with a '(needs description)' suffix + a data-attr flag, and have the AI content auditor / fidelity banner count generic-alt images as unresolved rather than letting axe presence clear them.

### F15 [medium] Table header scope is assigned POSITIONALLY (first row → col) in both stampers — transposed / row-header tables get the wrong header axis, mis-announcing every cell, with PDF/UA-1 still declared

- **Area:** Tables / header scope correctness
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** _stampThScopeGeometryAware at :984 (despite the name, header-row/row-0 => col, later-row <th> => row); Stage-5a Pass 2 promotes an all-textual first row to scope=col; Stage-5b-full only corrects transposed/crosstab when Gemini confidence >=0.7.
- **Detail:** Both scope-stamping paths are purely positional, not geometry-true. _stampThScopeGeometryAware assigns scope=col to any <th> in the first row/thead and scope=row to later rows. Stage-5a does the same in DOM form and, for tables with no <th>, PROMOTES the entire first row to scope=col when its cells are all-textual. For a TRANSPOSED data table (header axis is the LEFT column) or a row-header-only matrix, this assigns the wrong scope, so a screen reader announces the wrong header for every cell (a gradebook reads a student-name row label as a column header). The only correct handling for transposed layouts is Stage-5b-full, requiring a Gemini call with confidence >=0.7 — so without AI (throttled/offline/sub-threshold) the table ships mis-scoped. Because cells DO carry a scope attribute, axe's th-has-data-cells/scope-attr checks pass and the table is not flagged; PDF/UA-1 is unaffected. This actively mis-announces rather than merely omitting.
- **Trigger:** Remediate a transposed table where column 0 holds the headers ('Name','Grade','Date') down the left and each subsequent column is a record, with no <th> tags. Stage-5a promotes the all-textual first ROW to scope=col; every value is announced under the wrong header.
- **Suggested fix:** Before promoting/stamping scope=col, test the orthogonal hypothesis: if the FIRST COLUMN is uniformly textual/non-numeric and the first row is mixed/numeric, treat it as row-header (scope=row). Where ambiguous and no AI verdict is available, leave scope unset and flag for review rather than guessing an axis.

### F16 [medium] AcroForm fields and link annotations are appended at the END of the structure tree regardless of visual position — fillable IEPs/permission slips get all prose first, then all fields, breaking logical reading order while still declaring PDF/UA-1

- **Area:** Tagged-PDF output / form fields + reading order
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** combinedK = structElemRefs.concat(pageElemRefs).concat(fieldElemRefs).concat(linkAnnotElemRefs) (~:19962). Stage-3 form tagging ~:19599-19681; Stage-3b link tagging ~:19690-19739. _COUNTABLE includes Link but NOT Form (~:20325).
- **Detail:** Form-field /Form StructElems and /Link StructElems are concatenated AFTER all body content, then parented under the single /Document wrapper — top-level siblings placed last, not nested where the widget visually appears. For the project's exact target documents (a fillable IEP, permission slip, 'Student Name: [field] Date: [field]' worksheet), a screen-reader user traversing in logical order hears the entire prose, then a trailing block of bare form fields divorced from their labels — a PDF/UA-1 §7.18 / logical-reading-order failure. The PDF/UA-1 declaration is still written (orphan walk checks linkage only), and /Form is absent from _COUNTABLE so an unlinked form widget cannot even veto the declaration. Links share the same end-of-tree defect.
- **Trigger:** Tag an AcroForm PDF where fields are interleaved with text (an IEP signature page: 'Parent signature: [sig] Date: [date] Relationship: [text]'). The tagged output reads all prose, then dumps the fields at the very end detached from the in-context prompts; PDF/UA-1 still shows declared.
- **Suggested fix:** Insert each /Form and /Link StructElem into the per-page Sect/content sequence at the position matching the widget/annotation's (y,x) location (the same reading-order positioning Stage-4 needs), rather than concatenating as trailing siblings. Add Form to _COUNTABLE so an unlinked form widget vetoes the declaration.

### F17 [medium] One-click 'Revert last command' restores prior HTML but never re-scores — headline score and remaining-issues keep describing the reverted-away document

- **Area:** Scoring/Audit honesty — Workbench _preCmdHtml revert
- **File:** view_pdf_audit_source.jsx
- **Location:** Workbench revert onClick at :10258: setPdfFixResult(p => ({...p, accessibleHtml: p._preCmdHtml, _preCmdHtml:null, _lastMiniAudit:null, _lastCmdDiff:null, _lastTableReadback:null })) — clears command-diff but leaves afterScore/verificationAudit/axeAudit/issueResolution untouched and does NOT call _reauditAndScore. Contrast _revertPalette (:3237) which DOES re-audit.
- **Detail:** Every targeted-edit path snapshots _preCmdHtml then runs _reauditAndScore so the score reflects the new HTML. The Workbench revert does the inverse mutation (accessibleHtml := _preCmdHtml) but skips re-scoring, so the stored accessibleHtml is the pre-command document while the displayed afterScore/verificationAudit.issues still reflect the post-command document that was just discarded. If the reverted command improved the score, the teacher sees an inflated score for a document that no longer has those fixes; if it regressed, a depressed score. _revertPalette correctly re-audits after reverting — an inconsistency. Deterministic and user-initiated (lower than the in-flight race) but still shows a score that doesn't match the shipped bytes.
- **Trigger:** Run an Expert Workbench command (or any targeted edit setting _preCmdHtml) that changes the score, then click '↩ Revert last command'. The headline score and remaining-issue count remain at the post-command values.
- **Suggested fix:** After the revert setState, call await _reauditAndScore(p._preCmdHtml) (mirroring _revertPalette), or at minimum set a _scoreStale flag the UI surfaces as 'Re-run audit to refresh' so the displayed number isn't presented as current.

### F18 [medium] Plain-HTML and standard-ePub exports ship inserted-table editor chrome (× / + Row − Row buttons) and a live <script> into the deliverable; DOCX/ODT/DAISY/EPUB-MO strip it

- **Area:** Export fidelity / deliverable honesty (HTML + standard ePub)
- **File:** view_pdf_audit_source.jsx
- **Location:** Inserted smart-table block embeds '<button class="allo-block-remove">×</button>' + '<div class="allo-block-controls">+ Row − Row + Col − Col</div>' at :2579. HTML export buttons (:9149 via _wrapAsReaderApp which only injects a reader bar + script; :9876 raw DOCTYPE prepend) and the standard ePub (whole-doc XMLSerializer) ship accessibleHtml without _isControlEl stripping. _isControlEl (:164/:170) is used only by _htmlToDocxSpec/_collectMoSegments (:626).
- **Detail:** Inserting a smart table writes interactive remediation chrome (remove '×' button, '+ Row − Row + Col − Col' controls) directly into accessibleHtml. DOCX/ODT/DAISY strip it via _isControlEl, and EPUB-MO via _collectMoSegments. But both HTML export buttons and the standard (non-media-overlay) ePub export ship accessibleHtml without any stripper — and _wrapAsReaderApp additionally injects an executable <script>. So a teacher who uses the Smart Table builder and downloads HTML or plain ePub distributes a file with visible '×' / '+ Row' affordances (contenteditable=false but rendered and AT-readable) where the DOCX/ODT of the same doc is clean. Anything that reached accessibleHtml without renderJsonToHtml escaping (pasted designMode content, an embedded iframe, a rawhtml block) also ships unfiltered on the HTML path only.
- **Trigger:** In the preview, insert a Smart Table, then Export → HTML (or the standard ePub button). The downloaded file shows a '×' remove button and a '+ Row − Row + Col − Col' control bar baked into the page.
- **Suggested fix:** Add '.allo-block-controls, .allo-block-remove' to the strip selectors and route the HTML and standard-ePub exports through getPdfPreviewHtml() / the same _isControlEl chrome-strip (and DOMPurify where available) the other formats use, before wrapping.

### F19 [medium] 'Analysis + Verified' meta label overclaims an AI search self-assessment as independent verification

- **Area:** Document Builder / generation honesty
- **File:** generate_dispatcher_source.jsx
- **Location:** metaInfo = isSearchActive ? t('meta.analysis_verified') : t('meta.analysis_standard') (:2372); recommendation copy 'surface AI-verified facts' (:238); performDeepVerification populates analysisData.accuracy.verifiedFacts (rehydrated :2340).
- **Detail:** When a source-text analysis runs with search active, the result is tagged t('meta.analysis_verified') → 'Analysis + Verified' (translated as 'authenticated/verified' across packs). The underlying mechanism is performDeepVerification — Gemini self-reporting a Google-Search factual check and emitting a 'Verified Facts' list. That is an AI search-grounded SELF-assessment, not independent verification, yet the badge presents it as 'Verified' to the teacher, and the recommendation copy ('surface AI-verified facts') reinforces it. The bibliography's verify-before-citing caveat doesn't propagate here. For the UMaine measurement-honesty posture this 'Verified' framing on AI-generated fact lists is exactly the overclaim smell the project is trying to eliminate.
- **Trigger:** Run 'Analyze Source Text' with the accuracy/search check enabled; the resulting card is badged 'Analysis + Verified' over a Gemini self-reported facts list.
- **Suggested fix:** Relabel to 'Analysis + AI fact-check' / 'AI-checked (not independently verified)' and carry the same unverified caveat used in the bibliography; reword the 'AI-verified facts' recommendation copy.

### F20 [low] RTL / bidi direction is dropped from every Office and DAISY export — RTL translations and RTL-source docs export left-to-right

- **Area:** Export fidelity — language direction (DOCX/ODT/DTBook)
- **File:** view_pdf_audit_source.jsx
- **Location:** _htmlToDocxSpec (:150) extracts only lang (:153), never dir; spec has no dir/rtl field. _buildDocxBlobFromSpec sets run language but no bidi/rightToLeft. _htmlToOdtContentXml and _htmlToDtbookXml read spec.lang only. Zero bidi/rightToLeft tokens in the file; the only dir refs are the unrelated _moveTagEl(el, dir) param.
- **Detail:** The translation companion is explicitly RTL-aware (translateAccessibleHtml returns rtl; the compare pane renders direction:rtl and labels '(RTL)'), and a source PDF can itself be Arabic/Hebrew/Farsi/Urdu. But the shared export block model _htmlToDocxSpec captures only lang, never document/per-block dir, and none of the three serializers (DOCX, ODT content.xml, DTBook) emit any direction. DOCX needs bidi/rtl run+paragraph props; ODF needs writing-mode/text-align; DTBook needs dir. So a teacher who translates a notice to Arabic and downloads Word/ODT/DAISY gets left-to-right text with left alignment — visually wrong and semantically incorrect for AT in those formats. Only the typeset-tagged-PDF path warns about RTL.
- **Trigger:** Translate a remediated document to Arabic/Hebrew/Urdu (or remediate an RTL-language PDF), then export to .docx/.odt/DAISY. Output reads left-to-right with left alignment.
- **Suggested fix:** Thread the document/block dir (and the translation companion's rtl flag) into the _htmlToDocxSpec block model and emit bidi/rightToLeft (DOCX), writing-mode/text-align (ODT), dir (DTBook); or minimally surface a 'these formats do not preserve right-to-left layout — use HTML/PDF for RTL' note (parity with the existing tagged-PDF RTL warning).

### F21 [medium] URL-import silently sends the teacher-typed URL to three third-party proxies (jina.ai / corsproxy.io / allorigins.win) with no disclosure

- **Area:** Security / FERPA — undisclosed egress beyond Gemini
- **File:** utils_pure_source.jsx
- **Location:** fetchAndCleanUrl (:317): r.jina.ai (:438), corsproxy.io (:455/:491), api.allorigins.win (:465/:511). Direct mirror in view_pdf_audit_source.jsx:3396 (allorigins/raw). UI call sites: Doc-Builder 'Import from URL' and web-audit 'Fetch'. No disclosure copy.
- **Detail:** The FERPA story rests on 'content goes ONLY to Gemini.' URL-import breaks the envelope: the user-typed URL is sent to r.jina.ai, corsproxy.io, and api.allorigins.win — independent third parties NOT covered by any Gemini/Workspace DPA — which fetch the page server-side and can log the URL and body. The web-audit 'Fetch' button does the same directly via allorigins. None of the surfaces disclose this. The fetched content is a public page (lower severity than document egress), but the URL ITSELF egresses, and a teacher can paste a tokenized/PII-bearing link (a student-portal URL with a name or session token in the query string) that lands in those proxies' logs — exactly the 'no data leaves the browser' overclaim flagged for review, reachable from two in-product buttons.
- **Trigger:** In Doc Builder, paste any URL into 'Import from URL' and click Fetch → fetchAndCleanUrl sends https://corsproxy.io/?<url> then https://api.allorigins.win/raw?url=<url>. The URL goes to corsproxy/allorigins, not Google.
- **Suggested fix:** Add a one-line disclosure next to the URL inputs ('The link you paste is sent to a third-party fetch proxy; don't paste links containing student info'). Prefer the Gemini urlContext path as PRIMARY so the URL stays within the Gemini envelope, falling back to the public proxies only with the disclosure shown.

### F22 [low] Reliability panel labels heuristics 'Auditor Consistency (ICC-like)' / 'Cronbach α-like' with a 95% CI / SEM and an 'Excellent agreement' verdict; serialized field names icc/cronbachAlpha carry no disclaimer

- **Area:** Scoring/Audit honesty (psychometric framing for a gatekeeper audience)
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** icc = Math.max(0, 1 - rawSD/50) and cronbachAlpha = CV+pairwise hybrid computed at ~:7788-7821; rendered at view_pdf_audit_source.jsx:4954-4988 ('Auditor Consistency', '95% CI', 'SEM', 'Cronbach α-like', '✅ Excellent agreement'). Serialized via saveProjectToFile / batch CSV with literal icc/cronbachAlpha keys.
- **Detail:** icc/cronbachAlpha are explicitly NOT the textbook statistics (custom 1−SD/50 index and a CV+pairwise hybrid; names kept for save-file back-compat). In-app tooltips are now honest ('not textbook ICC', 'not textbook Cronbach's α', 'varied persona prompts, same model'). Residual exposure: (1) the rendered LABELS still read 'Auditor Consistency (ICC-like)', '95% CI', 'SEM', plus an 'Excellent agreement — auditors highly consistent' verdict — a hurried UMaine reviewer sees ICC/Cronbach/CI/SEM and an 'Excellent agreement' badge before reading the disclaiming tooltips; (2) any path that serializes/exports these fields carries the literal icc/cronbachAlpha names with no tooltip — the 'AdminBrief still says ICC/Cronbach' debt. This is psychometric framing of single-model prompt-sensitivity (N re-prompts of the same model).
- **Trigger:** Run a multi-auditor initial audit (auditorCount>1) → expand 'Auditor Consistency' → the panel shows ICC-like/α-like/95% CI/SEM and an 'Excellent agreement' verdict. Or export a batch report / save a project and inspect the serialized icc/cronbachAlpha fields.
- **Suggested fix:** Rename the rendered labels to drop psychometric terms ('Re-prompt spread', 'Reproducibility index (custom)') and reword the verdict to 'auditors reproduced this score consistently'. For serialization emit aliased keys (rePromptReproducibilityIndex) or attach a one-line disclaimer alongside the numbers in exported reports.

### F23 [medium] Before→after 'gain' compares a deliberately-skeletal HTML reconstruction (empty <title>, no lang, single <main>) against a fully-formed remediated shell — part of the reported delta is the harness, not remediation

- **Area:** Scoring/Audit honesty (before/after methodology asymmetry)
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** Baseline minimalHtml at ~:7975-7990 ('<html><head><title></title></head><body><main>'+bodyHtml) vs final score on the full remediated shell (with lang/title/landmarks); gain reported in the success toast (~:17142) and batch report.
- **Detail:** The BEFORE deterministic baseline runs axe+EA on minimalHtml with an EMPTY <title>, NO lang attribute, and only a bare <main>. axe reliably flags document-title (empty) and html-has-lang on this skeleton, dragging the baseline-axe score down. The AFTER axe runs on the full remediated shell with lang, a real <title>, and landmarks. A meaningful slice of the headline 'gain' (surfaced as '+N points' in the toast and batch CSV) is an artifact of the reconstruction harness adding title/lang/landmarks the BEFORE skeleton was constructed without — not remediation work on the source content. A reviewer reads '46 → 92 (+46)' as remediation efficacy when part of the delta is the harness.
- **Trigger:** Audit then remediate any text PDF. The initial baseline scores a no-title/no-lang/single-main skeleton; the final scores a full lang/title/landmark shell. The '+N' improvement includes the harness-introduced document-title and html-has-lang fixes.
- **Suggested fix:** Build the baseline minimalHtml with the same shell scaffolding the output uses (placeholder <title>, lang from detected source language, landmarks) so before/after measures the SAME class of document, OR disclose in the gain copy that the baseline is a minimal reconstruction and the deterministic delta partly reflects shell scaffolding.

### F24 [medium] Synthesized axe-only verification produces a clean-looking WCAG issues list; a consumer reading verification.issues without checking the synthesized flag treats axe output as a full WCAG verification

- **Area:** Scoring/Audit honesty (degraded-mode object shape)
- **File:** doc_pipeline_source.jsx
- **Location:** Synthesized verification fallback at ~:16444-16462 (maps axe violations to {wcag, issue, severity}, passes:[], synthesized:true); consumed as verificationAudit (~:16997) and rendered as the issues list; report-render reads verificationAudit.score (~:17782).
- **Detail:** When BOTH the loop's AI verify and the final audit return null, the pipeline synthesizes verification from axe-core. _aiDegraded picks up verification.synthesized so the HEADLINE correctly drops to deterministic-only (good). BUT the verification OBJECT now looks exactly like a real AI WCAG verification: a populated issues[] with wcag codes and severities; the only signal these are axe-derived is the summary string + the boolean synthesized flag. The remediation UI renders verification.issues as 'remaining content issues'; any code path or export that reads verificationAudit.issues/wcag/severity without gating on synthesized presents axe-core's blind-to-PDF-bytes, blind-to-semantics output as the content/WCAG verification layer — honest at the headline, fragile at the issues-list/export layer.
- **Trigger:** Remediate during a sustained Gemini outage so both the loop verify and final audit return null but axe succeeds. The verification panel/issue list shows axe rules styled as WCAG content-verification issues; an export consumer reading verificationAudit.issues without the synthesized check labels them a full audit.
- **Suggested fix:** Stamp each synthesized issue with _synthesized:true (not just the parent object) and have issue-list/export renderers branch on it to label 'axe-core automated (AI semantic verification unavailable)'. Or give synthesized verification a distinct type so no consumer can read it as a completed WCAG content verification.
